data.description:
Toyo Tanso (TSE:5310) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of ¥12.1b and basic EPS of ¥44.49, capping a year in which trailing twelve month revenue came in at ¥46.2b and EPS at ¥260.53.   • Over the last 12 months, Toyo Tanso generated ¥46,189 million in revenue and ¥5,464 million in net income, which works out to an 11.8% net profit margin compared with 18.8% a year earlier.
  • Critics highlight margin pressure as a key bearish point, and the numbers back that concern, with quarterly net income moving from ¥2,771 million in Q4 FY 2024 to ¥933 million in Q4 FY 2025 and trailing net margin stepping down alongside that shift.
    • This period also saw trailing EPS move from 474.91 yen to 260.53 yen, which lines up with the idea that profitability has been under pressure across the year.

  • What stands out for the bullish story is that this valuation premium sits next to earnings forecast to grow about 17.1% per year and revenue about 9.6% per year, compared with JP market forecasts of 9% and 5%.

Price-to-Earnings of 74.1x: Is it justified?

At ¥5,858 per share, Ezaki Glico is trading on a P/E of 74.1x, which stands out given its recent earnings track record and sector comparisons.

The P/E ratio tells you how much investors are paying today for each unit of current earnings. For a food and beverage group like Ezaki Glico, a high P/E often implies the market is placing a strong emphasis on earnings improvement rather than what has just been reported.
